Hello there. How to sell the offers. So the first part of selling is knowing what you were selling. So it kind of made sense for us to begin with what the offers are, what's included in them, and kind of how to position them in your minds. So how to sell the offers. So you can see on the list on the top here free 42-day detox, free 42-day challenge, free 6-week challenge, free 6-week transformation, all of those versions as non-free as in just 42-day detox, 42-day challenge, 6-week challenge, etc. and other offers in the sales process. So ultimately like the reason I have all these examples is that the widget that we use on the front end to bring someone the door is more or less wrapping paper for the same present. And what I mean by that is that like what we are bringing them in with will constantly change from a headline and offer standpoint, but it will still ultimately have the same components to it. You're still always going to have fitness, nutrition, and accountability. And we're going to over deliver in all three of these regards so that we can maximize the likelihood that that person is successful. And in the selling process those will be the three pillars that we will explain to them. And typically the reason most people have failed in the past is because they're lacking one of those three things. Or one of those three things was not done, you know, or not executed properly, right? Like if you had perfect accountability then they would never have fallen off in the first place. If they had a perfect nutrition plan then they would continue to eat that because they enjoyed how it tasted. It was easy and understandable and they could live with it, right? So like it's very easy to show someone where the deficit exists and then show how we can fix that. Underneath here I have a few different questions that a lot of people ask related to the offers that we recommend most people start with. And I want to break down each one of them individually. So why is it six weeks or 42 days? We can run and we have Jim successfully run 28 day programs, our HCP program which is a legacy program for those of you who continue on into legacy is how we sell you know $1,000 to $3,000 front end packages that are 12 weeks long. And so the duration of the offers ultimately is fluid. We have just seen that for general population for larger training six weeks slash 42 days tends to be the best converting offer and we believe me have spent a lot of money testing this. And so I think part of that comes down to the psychology of six weeks being long enough that someone believes they can achieve a significant result short enough they believe they can achieve it. And I think that's why six weeks is kind of like the perfect amount of time. It has converted for the last six and a half years to my knowledge. We still have not need to change that. And if you think about the fitness industry in general, if you are marketing for a gym you're going to be giving away a duration based program. Whether it's 21 days, seven days, 14 days, 28 days, six weeks, you know 90 days, whatever. It's always going to be a duration based program because that's ultimately what it is. It's typically giving a defined period of time and then followed by continuity and that is more or less the gym industry. So the question of like when will things like this expire? Like platforms will expire in terms of what media we are buying. Headlines and copy will expire but the concept of giving X amount of time away on the front end or having an offer that is duration based on the front end for a gym, if you look in the last 30, 40 years hasn't really changed. So I don't really see that as something that will fundamentally change in the long run. So that is why six weeks it's not that we're married to it, it's just that it just converts the best when we spend across different promotions. So why free? If you noticed on the page before this I had free 42 day detox, free 60 challenge, free all that kind of stuff. And the reason we recommend using the word free is because free converts the best. So six weeks converts best, free converts best. You know Dana Ariely and predictably Rational talked about how he had a Godiva chocolate and a Hershey kiss and when the Hershey kiss was a penny and Godiva chocolate was 15 cents most people bought the Godiva chocolate. But as soon as he decreased both prices by one penny and it went to 14 cents for the Godiva chocolate and free for the Hershey kiss, way more people took the Hershey kiss and it's because the power of free is still like it's deeply ingrained in our psychology. And that's why there's also more laws around how you can say free. And so this is the explanation from a marketing standpoint why we say free, which is it's going to convert better, it's going to get more leads at lower price and ultimately allow you to help more people. Now, how do we do this for free? So I want to be very clear here. You always need to obey FTC regulations, so you can be a compliant advertiser, you never want to go against that. And so I just want to be very upfront about that. So there are lots of ways that someone can use this offer to get it for free and ultimately make the offer even more irresistible in my opinion to a prospect that's coming in the door. And so the first way that someone can get it for free is by hitting a goal. And so the gyms that we use typically pick between a 10% body mass loss goal saying so that kind of adjusts for everyone. So if you have a 130 pound rural, she would need to lose 13 pounds. If you have a 200 pound guy, you need to lose 20. Same thing. So it's 10%, right? That is one option. And it does simplify things. When we ran this at our facilities and what I would say the majority of people do is they run two options, meaning you can get it for free if you lose 25 pounds or 6% body fat. And basically the 6% body fat allows you to take on people who are lighter. And then by extension, when they are like they don't need to necessarily lose weight, they just need to lose body fat per cent. So that allows you to take on like if you had a 130 pound girl who's 5'8", she doesn't really need to lose 13 pounds. And so or at least very likely she might need to lose 13 pounds of fat, but she won't lose 13 pounds of scale weight if she hasn't been exercising because as soon as she starts exercising, she'll start gaining muscle. And so, and I mean really lean body mass, not muscle, but I'm not going to get into it. Point being, the way that this works is that they would put down an adiposit that they're, you know, dedicated doing the program. They're going to put skin in the game. And then after they hit their goal, they get the money back. All right. That's one way they can get it free. The other way they can get it for free is with a money back satisfaction guarantee. And this is something that I hit on all the time in when I trained our sales guys. And honestly, how I closed a ton, a ton, a ton of sales is just by giving people money back guarantee, which is just that if I don't deliver the service that you feel like you deserve, I'll give you your money back. No questions asked. And I want to tell you like very truthfully, with six locations and 4,000 sales, I only had two people take me up on that in the entire duration of the time that I ran that offer. So if you are confident in your fulfillment and you really, really try hard to help people, they've like, they basically won't do it. They will not ask for their money back. All right. You obviously have to provide that for them if they ask. Okay. Like I want to be very clear about that. If you do not do that, then you are practicing deceptive advertising and just a bunch of stuff that you don't want. So I want to be clear, if someone hits the goal, any goal, you need to give them their money back. Or, you know, give them the option to credit it as a membership, whatever, but you need to uphold your side here. I thought that this went without saying and I never liked punishing the many for the few. But we had a very small handful of gyms not hear this out clearly with this message that I'm telling you. And so I want to be very clear here. If you market something is free, you need to explain to them why it is free and how it works for them to get it for free. And you need to honor that. 99% of gyms don't have this issue. But I want to be extremely clear so that even the 1% hear me loud and clear. Okay. That is how it is free. There's other ways you can get it, you know, for free, you can make it like sort of a bribe offer will give you this program if you become a member. So it's like you're getting the whole program for free as you join and become a member. There's lots of different ways to do it. That being said, what I recommend for everyone, especially the first time around is market it, go for the deposit sale, meaning when they come in, explain to them that they will, if they hit the goal, they'll be able to get the money back and use it as credit towards staying or money back straight up. And then close the deal by saying, you know, you, no matter what, you're going to have a money back guarantee so that if you feel like we didn't provide the service that you deserve, we'll give your money back anyways. Okay. So that is how we can market this is free and be compliant. This is in no way legal advice. I always check with your legal counsel, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah of what the legal leads you're supposed to say here. But essentially always be a compliant advertiser, always follow the rules. It's just not worth it. Okay. So how do the offers differ? So what I mean by that is how does a 42 day detox differ from a 42 day challenge, differ from a 42 day transformation program for six week challenge from a 28 day challenge with 12 week program. The big secret is that they don't, right? It's the same wrapping paper, sorry, different wrapping paper on the same box. It's still going to be the same three pillars, fitness, nutrition, accountability. And so you can use the selling process no matter what the front end offer is, whatever the widget is that's attracting the person that is converting highest in your market at this point. It does not matter. The point is that that's what's going to generate the lead and journey at the inquiry. But ultimately, people are trying to lose weight. And so that's what all of this is going to be about. Free versus non free. This is something that just exists within our community. So I just want to take two seconds to explain it. Free versus non free free, I explain how the free works non free is basically marketing everything identical to what I just said, except not using the word free. So just having 42 day detox just having 42 day transformation program, 42 day challenge, etc. Everything works the same. The only thing that would be different here is that obviously if you're not marketing free, you don't have to do any of these, you know, money, there's no, at least to my knowledge, there is no obligation. I mean, it's your marketing program. And if someone inquires about it and doesn't say free anywhere, then you can charge them like you normally would. Or you know, you explain how the program works and explain what the fees cost. Some people prefer that that's entirely up to you that is your prerogative. The only difference is that free will obviously get a higher amount of leads and a cheaper cost. Some people will say that non free brings in higher quality leads, which I don't, I don't argue with. But in my experience, you'll get more leads, you'll make more sales, you'll get a higher ROI in total with free, even accounting for the fact that you'll get some crazy people. So I just want to be clear about that and kind of explain the difference between those two things. And that concludes our section on how the offers work.
